discrepancy_analysis:
A. DATA LOADED
   - Paper: PMC10172395 (Suppiah et al., Nat Commun 2023, 14:2696)
   - Relevant figure: Fig. 1h (CNV heatmap generated from CONUMEE on methylation data)
   - Reported source: MOESM4 clinical data (n = 108 methylation samples; MPNST-G2 n = 8)
   - Model analysis used GSE207207 methylation beta values / IDATs via a Python CONUMEE-substitute.

B. COHORT DEFINITION
   - Paper text (p. 2): 'MPNST-G2 exhibited fewer chromosomal alterations (average 8.9 chromosomes affected), including loss of chromosome 1p (50%), 6p (62.5%), and 9p (62.5%), and gain of chromosome 12q (50%).'
   - Denominator: 8 MPNST-G2 samples (high-grade MPNSTs clustered into methylation subclass G2).

C. VARIABLE CODING
   - Outcome: binary call of chromosome-arm 12q gain per sample.
   - Paper method: CNV segments and arm-level calls produced by the R/Bioconductor CONUMEE algorithm (ref. 15 in paper) applied directly to EPIC IDATs, which uses a normal/control reference, GC-/probe-density normalization, circular binary segmentation, and CONUMEE's default thresholds for gain/loss (typically |log2 ratio| > ~0.1, often calibrated to the reference set's noise distribution).
   - Model method: Python 'CONUMEE-substitute' computing log2 ratios directly from beta/intensity data without the full CONUMEE normalization + segmentation pipeline and using an unspecified threshold. Highest observed 12q log2 was +0.084, which failed whatever gain threshold was applied.

D. COMPUTATION
   - Reported: gain of 12q in 4/8 MPNST-G2 samples = 50%.
   - Model: 0/8 MPNST-G2 samples called as 12q gain (max log2 ratio +0.084, below threshold).
   - Relative difference = 100% (0% vs 50%). This is a MAJOR discrepancy on the frequency scale (absolute difference 50 percentage points; 4 sample flips needed).

E. RESULT
   - Model: 0% (0/8)
   - Reported: 50% (4/8)
   - Discrepancy: DISCREPANT.

F. ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION
   - The paper is explicit (Methods + Fig. 1h legend): CNV calls come from the R package CONUMEE (Hovestadt & Zapatka), which (a) uses a curated normal reference panel for background normalization, (b) applies probe-level GC/intensity corrections, (c) runs circular binary segmentation (CBS), and (d) uses segment-level log2-ratio thresholds (default around ~0.1, sometimes tuned) to call chromosomal-arm-level gains/losses. CONUMEE's default reporting also considers the proportion of an arm covered by a gain segment.
   - The model used a Python CONUMEE-substitute that omits one or more of: (a) the properly normalized reference panel, (b) CBS segmentation, (c) CONUMEE's arm-level aggregation rule. Without these, gene-level raw log2 ratios are shallower than CONUMEE's segment-level estimates. A maximum 12q log2 of +0.084 is consistent with under-normalized / unsegmented data: real copy-number gains seen by CONUMEE often have segment-level log2 ratios of 0.15-0.4 but raw per-probe medians of <0.1 because averaging across non-gained probes on the arm dilutes the signal.
   - There is no indication of data error; the paper's reported 50% is robustly supported by Fig. 1h's CNV heatmap and by the synopsis in Fig. 6 which explicitly lists '12q' gain as characteristic of MPNST-G2.

G. CLASSIFICATION
   - Root cause: methodological - STATISTICAL_METHOD / algorithmic substitution. Specifically, using a Python approximation instead of the R CONUMEE pipeline yields systematically shallower log2 ratios and misses arm-level gain calls.
   - This is not a cohort/variable/data-mapping problem; the denominator (n = 8 MPNST-G2) and variable definition (12q gain) match the paper.
proposed_fix: Run the paper's actual pipeline: use the R/Bioconductor 'conumee' package (recipe: /home/klkehl/.../skills/analyze-data/recipes/R/conumee_cnv.R) on the EPIC IDATs from GSE207207 with an appropriate normal reference set (e.g., the benign neurofibroma G6/G7 samples or CONUMEE's bundled controls), perform circular binary segmentation, and apply CONUMEE's default arm-level gain threshold. This should recover the segmented log2 ratios on 12q (expected ~0.15-0.3 in the 4 affected MPNST-G2 samples) and reproduce the 50% gain frequency. Alternatively, use the paper's Source Data for Fig. 1h (if available) to read CNV calls directly rather than re-deriving them.
